problem_addressed: Conventional geothermal reservoir modeling approaches are inefficient for uncertainty quantification as they require estimating unknown model parameters before making predictions.
core_claim: Data Space Inversion (DSI) provides a robust and efficient alternative for making predictions with quantified uncertainty by avoiding direct parameter estimation entirely.
key_contributions: Introduction of DSI as an alternative methodology for geothermal reservoir modeling that bypasses parameter estimation
Development of a modified DSI algorithm that directly samples from the posterior predictive distribution
Demonstration of DSI's accuracy and efficiency through two geothermal reservoir modeling case studies
novelty: Unlike conventional Bayesian approaches that first estimate model parameters before predicting, DSI works directly in the data space to approximate the posterior predictive distribution without any parameter estimation step.
method_summary: DSI constructs a surrogate mapping from Gaussian samples to the joint distribution of data and predictive quantities by approximating marginal CDFs, applying Cholesky factorization for Gaussian approximation, and using triangular transformations for posterior sampling.
method_components: Sample generation from prior predictive distribution using forward model simulations
Construction of piecewise linear CDF approximations for each data and prediction variable
Application of inverse transformation and Cholesky factorization to approximate joint distribution as Gaussian
Triangular transformation structure enabling direct posterior predictive sampling
key_innovation: The triangular transformation structure that allows direct sampling from the posterior predictive distribution without parameter estimation by leveraging the monotonicity and lower-triangular properties of the constructed surrogate mapping.
assumptions: The joint distribution of the transformed variables can be reasonably approximated as Gaussian
The transformation functions are strictly increasing on their respective domains
The covariance matrix is full rank (with possible regularization via identity matrix addition)
theoretical_grounding: Based on Marzouk et al. (2016) for the sampling properties of the triangular transformation, though no new proofs or bounds are presented in this paper.
title: Data space inversion for efficient predictions and uncertainty quantification for geothermal models
